jokes.text: I'm Phoebe Buffay, and I have babies coming out.
jokes.setup: Phoebe introducing herself casually
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery about 'babies coming out'
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - blunt, matter-of-fact delivery of something momentous. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Which of you is the father? None of them are. The father is my brother.
jokes.setup: Medical staff assuming one of the friends is the father
jokes.punchline: Reveal that her brother is the father (surrogacy context)
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff that sounds scandalous until you know the surrogacy context. Classic Phoebe shocking people.

jokes.text: I'll miss watching you freak people out like that.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's previous shocking statement
jokes.punchline: Friend acknowledging this is typical Phoebe behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that acknowledges Phoebe's habit of shocking people.

jokes.text: Can't wait to see you. Please don't hurt me!
jokes.setup: Sweet message to unborn babies
jokes.punchline: Honest fear about childbirth pain
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between maternal warmth and honest anxiety. Very Phoebe.

jokes.text: You're not wearing one. Oh, man! I did it again!
jokes.setup: Joey explains he went back for his jacket
jokes.punchline: He's not wearing it and realizes he forgot it again
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey absent-mindedness. Well-structured setup/payoff with good character integration.

jokes.text: She hit her head in the shower. Oh, my God. She's so stupid!
jokes.setup: Doctor can't come because she hit her head
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt, inappropriate response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - inappropriate bluntness in a stressful moment.

jokes.text: I don't see three kids coming out of your vagina!
jokes.setup: Friends trying to reassure Phoebe it will be okay
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's crude but accurate comeback
jokes.explanation: Sharp, crude comeback that's both funny and valid. Classic Phoebe directness with good shock value.

jokes.text: And so the miracle of birth begins. And I... You okay? Something hurts.
jokes.setup: Ross dramatically narrating the birth
jokes.punchline: Immediately interrupted by his own pain
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Ross's pompous narration immediately undercut.

jokes.text: It's sympathy pains. That's so sweet! I didn't know I cared that much.
jokes.setup: Ross experiencing pain during Phoebe's labor
jokes.punchline: Ross's self-absorbed surprise at his own empathy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross - making Phoebe's labor about himself while thinking he's being sweet.

jokes.text: And also, I love Fonzie.
jokes.setup: Professional doctor introduction and reassurance
jokes.punchline: Completely random Fonzie declaration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment. Perfect economy and timing, completely unexpected in professional context.

jokes.text: Don't worry. She told me about the LaMazda stuff. If you get the babies out by month's end... you get two percent financing.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to be helpful about birthing classes
jokes.punchline: Confuses Lamaze with car dealership financing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Frank's well-meaning but clueless character.

jokes.text: They're male nurses. Not in my head.
jokes.setup: Monica's excited reaction to hearing about cute nurses
jokes.punchline: Joey clarifies they're male, Monica's honest response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's fantasy vs reality. Efficient delivery.

jokes.text: There's always room for Jello.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting getting Jello with the nurses
jokes.punchline: Misquotes the advertising slogan
jokes.explanation: Mild Joey malapropism. Works for character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: How do you make that dirty? It's easy. I can do it with anything. Grandma's chicken salad...
jokes.setup: Monica asks how Joey makes Jello sound dirty
jokes.punchline: Joey claims he can make anything dirty, starts with innocent example
jokes.explanation: Standard Joey horniness joke. In character but predictable.

jokes.text: Now, girl baby, turn away. And boy babies... check it out! This is what naked women looked like when you were born.
jokes.setup: Ross showing historical items to unborn babies
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately showing magazine to babies based on gender
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Ross behavior. Good cringe comedy with clear character voice.

jokes.text: This one doesn't hurt eith... Yes, it does!
jokes.setup: Phoebe starting to say contraction doesn't hurt
jokes.punchline: Pain hits mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good timing on the pain interruption. Simple but effective physical comedy.

jokes.text: It was sweet at first, but now I think he's trying to steal my thunder.
jokes.setup: Ross having sympathy pains
jokes.punchline: Phoebe realizes he's making it about himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent character observation. Phoebe sees through Ross's self-absorption with perfect clarity.

jokes.text: Oh, you know, Fonzie dated triplets.
jokes.setup: Doctor examining Phoebe who's having triplets
jokes.punchline: Connects to Fonzie trivia inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the absurd character trait. Inappropriate timing makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I don't want some guy down there telling me I'm 'dilated-a-mundo.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe complaining about the Fonzie-obsessed doctor
jokes.punchline: Imagines Fonzie-style medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining medical terms with Fonzie speak. Very quotable and original.

jokes.text: Just goofing around.
jokes.setup: Ross saying they're nothing, just casual
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dismissive characterization of their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery that sets up Ross's later definition. Understated but effective.

jokes.text: Doogie!
jokes.setup: Young resident doctor trying to reassure Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe dismisses him with Doogie Howser reference
jokes.explanation: Easy pop culture reference joke. Works in context but lacks originality.

jokes.text: Oh, cool. You made him cry!
jokes.setup: Phoebe harshly dismissing young doctor
jokes.punchline: Joey's inappropriate enthusiasm about making someone cry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - inappropriately impressed by meanness. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Well, what else could it be? It's kidney stones. Or... Kidney stones. If it was something else? Well, that would be 'kidney stones.'
jokes.setup: Joey asking what else his condition could be
jokes.punchline: Doctor's deadpan insistence it's definitely kidney stones
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan medical humor. Doctor's certainty contrasts with Joey's hope for alternatives.

jokes.text: They'll be born on the Fonz's half-birthday.
jokes.setup: Doctor commenting on the timing of birth
jokes.punchline: Makes it about Fonzie's half-birthday
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag. 'Half-birthday' makes it even more absurd.

jokes.text: Just to clarify... I'm not Fonzie.
jokes.setup: Doctor's continued Fonzie references
jokes.punchline: Awkward clarification of obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant awkward clarification. Self-aware but doesn't break character's obliviousness.

jokes.text: I want to keep one.
jokes.setup: Phoebe talking about how special the surrogacy is
jokes.punchline: Shocking admission she wants to keep a baby
jokes.explanation: Shocking character moment with perfect economy. Subverts the sweet surrogacy narrative.

jokes.text: Three babies are a handful. Maybe they'll want to unload one.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to justify keeping a baby
jokes.punchline: Treating babies like unwanted merchandise
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Phoebe's twisted logic. 'Unload' is particularly callous and funny.

jokes.text: Tell him it's for you.
jokes.setup: Ross refusing to ask Frank for a baby
jokes.punchline: Phoebe suggests lying about who wants the baby
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Phoebe's scheme. Shows her manipulative side in desperate moment.

jokes.text: No, no, no, no. Nothing is going up, okay? Up is not an option. What's a urethra?
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining procedure involving going up the urethra
jokes.punchline: Joey's firm rejection followed by not knowing what it is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - strongly opposing something he doesn't understand. Great structure and character voice.

jokes.text: Undo it. Undo it.
jokes.setup: Doctor made mistake mentioning Mork meeting Fonzie
jokes.punchline: Desperate attempt to take back his words
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the character's obsession. Panic at mixing up his references.

jokes.text: You couldn't just do me a favor and, like, hold them in?
jokes.setup: Alice not arriving yet, babies coming soon
jokes.punchline: Frank's naive request to delay birth
jokes.explanation: Good Frank character moment - sweet but clueless about how birth works.

jokes.text: Sorry, I'm in the middle of the last favor you asked me to do.
jokes.setup: Frank asking Phoebe to hold in the babies
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dry reference to surrogacy as a 'favor'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe deadpan. Reduces the massive favor of surrogacy to casual terminology.

jokes.text: So, Dan... nurse, not a doctor, huh? Kind of girlie, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Ross meeting Dan, the male nurse dating Monica
jokes.punchline: Ross's inappropriately sexist comment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Ross's jealousy and poor social skills.

jokes.text: And it didn't feel 'girlie' during the Gulf War.
jokes.setup: Ross calling nursing girlie
jokes.punchline: Dan's serious military service comeback
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback that puts Ross in his place. Unexpected serious turn that works perfectly.

jokes.text: Thanks for doing that for us, by the way.
jokes.setup: Dan's serious Gulf War response
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward attempt to recover
jokes.explanation: Good awkward recovery attempt. Shows Ross trying to dig out of the hole he created.

jokes.text: I want the drugs, Ross! I do too! I do too!
jokes.setup: Phoebe in labor pain demanding drugs
jokes.punchline: Ross's desperate agreement about wanting drugs too
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross's sympathy pains subplot. His desperation matches Phoebe's.

jokes.text: Ready? Time to try peeing.
jokes.setup: Doctor telling Phoebe it's time to push
jokes.punchline: Ross's parallel declaration about his kidney stone
jokes.explanation: Excellent parallel structure. Ross's medical situation mirrors Phoebe's birth.

jokes.text: Yes! We got a baby boy! Frank Junior, Junior!
jokes.setup: First baby being born
jokes.punchline: Frank naming the baby after himself with 'Junior, Junior'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Frank's enthusiasm and the absurd name construction.

jokes.text: How does he look? So gross!
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking how the baby looks
jokes.punchline: Frank's honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Good honest moment. Frank's blunt assessment subverts expected sentiment.

jokes.text: I already had a baby. Leave me alone!
jokes.setup: Doctor saying she needs to push again for second baby
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's exhausted logic that she's done
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe logic - she's done her job, why continue? Relatable exhaustion.

jokes.text: Hey, where's his thing? Chandler's a girl! Oh, God! Kindergarten flashback.
jokes.setup: Expecting third baby to be boy named Chandler
jokes.punchline: Baby is girl, joke about Chandler's name being gender-confusing
jokes.explanation: Great callback to series-long joke about Chandler's name. Good misdirection with baby's gender.

jokes.text: There's 30 fingers and 30 toes.
jokes.setup: Frank reporting on the babies' health
jokes.punchline: Mathematical error - should be 30 total, not each
jokes.explanation: Good Frank moment showing his poor math skills in emotional moment.

jokes.text: Well, I have... and the technical definition is: Two friends who care a lot about each other... and have amazing sex... and just want to spend more time together.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning looking up 'goofing around' in dictionary
jokes.punchline: Ross's elaborate fake definition that describes a relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and callback. Ross's verbose attempt to redefine their relationship is very in character.

jokes.text: Don't do the dance. Right.
jokes.setup: Ross presumably starting to do some kind of victory celebration
jokes.punchline: Rachel stopping him, Ross's awkward agreement
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag we can infer. Ross's inappropriate celebration being shut down.

jokes.text: I know Alice's sister has a pool, but you lived in me.
jokes.setup: Phoebe talking about being the babies' favorite aunt
jokes.punchline: Her argument based on literal physical connection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe logic. Touching moment made funny by her unique reasoning about surrogacy.

jokes.text: Well, if you're going to cry...
jokes.setup: Babies presumably crying after her emotional speech
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact response to making babies cry
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment. Takes responsibility for making babies cry in typical direct way.

jokes.text: It works on you.
jokes.setup: Someone saying Chandler is a masculine name for a girl
jokes.punchline: Monica's dig at Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good quick comeback. Continues the running joke about Chandler's feminine traits.

jokes.text: Maybe they can girlie it up a bit, like Chandlerette. Alice called her Channy-fanny.
jokes.setup: Discussion of making Chandler's name more feminine
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous name variations
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay escalation. 'Channy-fanny' is particularly silly but not especially clever.
